description:
Parent epic fz-rh2.21; eli5.html is the design doc (Arc B); requires fz-rh2.21.6 landed (the two seams are already value-honest). This ticket removes numeric literal types from the compiler2 lattice — the data-model change — and gives literal numerics in TYPE position their decided meaning: WIDEN to integer()/float() with a compile-time warning (decided stance; matches Elixir, whose Module.Types.Descr cannot even express a numeric singleton: integer() and float() are single bitmap bits, while atoms keep finite/cofinite singleton sets — fz aligns exactly; atoms/bools/nil keep their singletons here too).

THE AXES FALL (src/compiler2/types/):
- descr.rs: the ints: IntSet and floats: FloatSet fields collapse to presence (fold into the BasicBits flags in bits.rs as INT/FLOAT presence bits, alongside the existing basic kinds). Delete the per-axis arms for ints/floats in union, intersect, neg/diff, is_empty, kinds_overlap, axis_free, looks_full, widen_literals, is_singleton_literal (becomes atom-only), as_int_singleton/as_float_singleton (return None, then delete callers per below).
- lit_set.rs: IntSet and FloatSet aliases leave; LiteralSet stays (atoms, brands, opaques, vars). F64Bits leaves the compiler2 lattice (check bits.rs).
- mod.rs (compiler2 Types): int_lit(n) becomes int(); float_lit becomes float() — keep the constructors as one-line widening shims is FORBIDDEN; instead update every compiler2-internal caller to call int()/float() directly and keep int_lit/float_lit ONLY where the shared SharedTypes trait (src/types/mod.rs) forces an impl — the OLD pipeline (src/types/concrete_types, src/type_infer, src/ir_planner) keeps its literal types until it dies, so compiler2's trait impl carries documented degenerate methods (int_lit ≡ int). Document this as the single permitted trait-coupling residue, with a pointer to the old pipeline's retirement as the removal trigger.
- DELETE ENTIRELY: widen_literal_flow and widen_for_recursive_spec_key (both become the identity once numerics cannot carry literal sets) including the free functions near the bottom of mod.rs, the call inside canonical_activation_key (src/compiler2/world.rs — the per-slot widen pass before convergence_class), and SemanticValue::from_observed's widen call (jobs/semantic.rs — the flow/observed split itself collapses in fz-rh2.21.8; here just make from_observed copy the type). refine_widen's literal-collapse fallback simplifies to plain union (its termination argument — bounded literal chains — holds vacuously once numerics cannot enter; atom chains are bounded by the program's finite atom set; structural depth is bounded by the fz-rh2.21.4 widening).
- format.rs/rendering: numeric axes render as int/float presence; no literal lists.
- runtime type predicates: Types::runtime_type_predicate's int/float set projections (mod.rs) collapse to kind-presence checks; the native_codegen int/float value-membership emission paths (native_codegen/prim.rs emit_i64_membership et al) become unreachable from compiler2-produced predicates — delete the compiler2-side production, keep the shared ObservedSet machinery (the old pipeline still produces value sets).

TYPE-POSITION LITERALS (the one observable semantic change — decided, documented):
- src/compiler2/resolve.rs (the TypeExpr::IntLit/FloatLit resolution arms): resolve to int()/float() and emit a WARNING diagnostic (new code in src/diag/codes.rs, e.g. TYPE_NUMERIC_LITERAL_WIDENED) with message stating the literal is not a type and widens to integer()/float(), suggesting a pattern or guard for value filtering. Keep TypeExpr::IntLit/FloatLit parsing (src/compiler2/type_expr.rs — Elixir surface compatibility) and the shared ResolvedTypeShape variants (src/specs/model.rs — shared with the old pipeline).
- BEHAVIOR CHANGE TO DOCUMENT IN THE COMMIT: @type digit :: 0 | 1 with fn f(x :: digit) previously compiled to a runtime value-membership check rejecting f(2); it now means integer() and accepts any int (with the compile warning). Grep fixtures2/ for affected fixtures (none were found at planning time; verify) and add a NEW fixture pinning the warning + the widened behavior.

TESTS: compiler2's lattice conformance suite (src/compiler2/types/types_test.rs, ~49 int_lit/float_lit sites: the refine_widen literal-collapse tests, ordering/meet tests, widen-shape tests, closure-capture meets, smoke tests) — rewrite to the new axis semantics (int_lit tests become int-presence tests or are deleted where they pinned exactly the removed machinery); world_test.rs and contract_test.rs literal-type sites; type_expr_test parse tests stay. Warning pinned via a diag-capture test. The full suite (epic baseline) must be green — in particular the fz-rh2.21.6 pins (reachability sets, map fixture types) now prove the pre-patches carried the weight.
Acceptance: green, zero warnings, one commit. LoC must go DOWN in src/compiler2/types/ — report the diffstat in the commit message.
title: lattice: remove numeric literal axes; type-position literals widen with warning
